message:
feat(tui): expand issue detail rich text with dependency graph, comments, and timeline sections

Significantly expand the issue detail RichText renderer with structured
sections for dependency visualization (blockers/dependents with status
chips), comment rendering, timeline/history view, and improved graph
metric display. Rotate old beads history snapshots to current
timestamps. Update issue statuses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","title":"Build reusable visual primitives: chips, badges, metric strips, semantic panels, and command hints with regression proof","description":"Purpose:
Create the shared presentational building blocks that let the redesigned screens stop rendering themselves as giant strings in generic boxes.

Required scope:
1. Add reusable primitives for status chips, priority badges, metric strips, panel headers, command chips, and section separators.
2. Add semantic panel treatments strong enough to support cockpit, analytics, graph, and investigation layouts.
3. Make these components narrow-width-safe and theme-aware.
4. Add render/snapshot coverage so the primitives themselves are stable and understandable.

Acceptance:
1. Rebuilt screens can be composed from shared primitives instead of bespoke text formatting.
2. The visual primitives materially improve hierarchy and scanability.
3. Component-level proof exists for the new primitives.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.209926749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:31:53.143997314Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:31:53.143467020Z","close_reason":"Wired all visual primitive families into live TUI surfaces in src/tui.rs and added focused runtime regression proof.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","frankentui","testing","tui","widgets"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.209926749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"FuchsiaAnchor: Implemented 9 reusable visual primitives in src/tui.rs with 12 regression tests:

1. status_chip() — coloured icon + abbreviated status text for all 9 known statuses
2. priority_badge() — coloured P0-P4 with clamping for out-of-range values
3. type_badge() — single-letter type icon (T/B/E/F/Q/D)
4. metric_strip() — compact label + mini bar + value display
5. blocker_indicator() — shows blocked count or unblocks count with colour coding
6. section_separator() — dim horizontal rule with 120-char max width cap
7. panel_header() — bold title with optional dim subtitle
8. label_chips() — bracketed coloured label tags inline
9. issue_scan_line() — dense single-line issue summary composing all of the above

All primitives return RichLine/RichSpan for composability, are width-safe and theme-aware. Acceptance criteria 1-3 satisfied (reusable primitives, narrow-width-safe, regression coverage). Remaining: AC4 (wire into actual rendering surfaces).","created_at":"2026-03-21T02:04:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","title":"Build grapheme-safe text, hyperlink, and terminal-native interaction primitives for the redesigned TUI","description":"Purpose:
Add the text-measurement and terminal-native interaction foundations that a flagship TUI needs before we scale up the redesign.

Background:
The current TUI is string-heavy. That makes it vulnerable to Unicode width bugs, truncation mistakes, and inconsistent wrapping as screens become richer. It also leaves terminal-native affordances like OSC-8 hyperlinks underused even though `bvr` has many natural link targets: issue IDs, repos, commits, docs, and external references.

Required scope:
- Adopt grapheme-safe, display-width-aware layout and truncation discipline for screen rendering.
- Add richer wrapping/truncation helpers appropriate for dense panels and narrow layouts.
- Introduce hyperlink handling for issue/detail/history/graph contexts where terminal links materially improve usability.
- Make links keyboard- and mouse-accessible rather than treating them as dead text.

Why it belongs early:
- It is easier to get width correctness and link affordances right before all screens are rebuilt.
- History and graph especially will benefit from terminal-native navigation once they stop being plain reports.

Acceptance:
- The redesign has a clear text-metrics policy suitable for Unicode-safe terminal rendering.
- Link-worthy entities can be rendered as actual terminal links where appropriate.
- Subsequent screen rebuilds can depend on these primitives rather than hacking one-off width logic.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Use display-width- and grapheme-aware text handling for layout-sensitive rendering paths.\n2. Avoid byte-length assumptions for truncation, wrapping, cursor placement, or alignment.\n3. Introduce terminal-native links only where they clearly improve operator flow, and ensure keyboard/mouse focus states are coherent.\n4. Keep the text and link primitives reusable across main, graph, and history surfaces.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead protects the redesign from subtle regressions that are easy to miss in ASCII-heavy terminals until users hit unusual titles, labels, repos, or external refs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The redesign has shared grapheme-safe truncation, wrapping, and alignment helpers suitable for dense panels.\n2. Link-capable surfaces can render real terminal links where appropriate without degrading keyboard-first workflows.\n3. Unit tests cover tricky Unicode and width cases.\n4. Integration or journey coverage proves that link and text affordances behave correctly inside at least one real screen.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.493702553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T03:38:45.873746453Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T03:38:45.873145876Z","close_reason":"All 4 acceptance criteria met. AC1: 6+ shared grapheme-safe helpers (fit_display, center_display, wrap_command_hints, truncate_display, display_width, command_hint_width). AC2: OSC-8 hyperlinks in Main/Graph/History with keyboard (o/y) and mouse (click/right-click) actions. AC3: 6 Unicode width tests (CJK double-width, emoji ZWJ, grapheme clusters). AC4: 7 integration tests proving link+text affordances in real screens (hyperlink rendering, mouse click/copy, detail scrolling). Multiple agents contributed over 5 days.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accessibility","frankentui","links","text","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.493702553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:48.609538600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"BlueRobin","text":"First concrete bd-2bpl.3.6.5 slice landed in src/tui.rs: added reusable CommandHint plus width-aware wrap_command_hints()/command_hint_line() helpers, and switched main/history footer hint generation onto styled RichText command hints instead of one raw ASCII sentence. The wrapping logic now packs whole hint groups by display width so narrow terminals get clean multi-line hint clusters. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib wrap_command_hints_preserves_groups_and_styles ; cargo test --lib main_footer_command_hints_wrap_across_multiple_lines ; cargo check --all-targets ; cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:04:54Z"},{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"BlueRobin","text":"Extended the active bd-2bpl.3.6.5 slice in src/tui.rs by moving terminal-native links onto a real operator surface. Main detail now renders through issue_detail_render_text() so a top-level External: row becomes an OSC-8 hyperlink when issue.external_ref is an http(s) URL, instead of dead text or a bottom-of-pane affordance that can scroll out of view. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib main_detail_renders_hyperlink_for_external_issue_reference ; cargo check --all-targets ; cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:08:25Z"},{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"BlueRobin","text":"Extended bd-2bpl.3.6.5 again in src/tui.rs: main detail external links now have a matching keyboard interaction path. Added selected_issue_external_ref_url()/should_open_selected_issue_external_ref() helpers, wired lowercase o to open the selected issue's http(s) external_ref only when in Main mode with detail focus, and preserved the existing o=open-filter behavior everywhere else. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib main_detail_open_shortcut_only_activates_for_detail_focus_with_http_ref ; cargo test --lib main_mode_o_keeps_open_filter_shortcut_without_external_ref ; cargo check --all-targets ; cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:11:46Z"},{"id":185,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added a main-detail clipboard path for external issue refs in src/tui.rs. Lowercase y now copies the selected issue's http(s) external_ref only when Main mode is focused on the detail pane, preserving existing y behavior elsewhere. Added focused regressions for guarded activation and the non-external-ref fallback. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib main_detail_copy_shortcut_only_activates_for_detail_focus_with_http_ref; cargo test --lib main_mode_y_without_external_ref_does_not_set_status_message; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:14:52Z"},{"id":186,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Made the main-mode command-hint footer context-aware in src/tui.rs so the new terminal-native external-ref actions are actually discoverable. The footer now uses main_footer_command_hints() and appends 'o open link' plus 'y copy link' only when Main mode is focused on the detail pane and the selected issue has an http(s) external_ref. Added render-surface regressions for both the visible and hidden cases. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib main_footer_shows_external_ref_commands_when_detail_link_is_available; cargo test --lib main_footer_hides_external_ref_commands_without_detail_link; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:17:18Z"},{"id":187,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Extended the main-detail external-ref surface in src/tui.rs so the hyperlink line itself advertises the available terminal-native actions. The rendered External: row now appends a dim '(o open, y copy)' affordance next to the OSC-8 link, which makes the keyboard path discoverable even before the footer is read. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib main_detail_renders_hyperlink_for_external_issue_reference; cargo check --all-targets (remote command succeeded; artifact retrieval hit a transient vanished-file warning afterward); cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:27:17Z"},{"id":190,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Extended the main detail render path in src/tui.rs so the primary issue-identity line is also self-describing. The first line now appends a dim '(C copy id)' affordance next to the selected issue header, which makes the existing copy shortcut discoverable directly at the point of use. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib main_detail_renders_hyperlink_for_external_issue_reference; cargo test --lib copy_issue_id_sets_status_msg; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-16T16:40:09Z"},{"id":192,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Extended the self-describing main-detail interaction surface in src/tui.rs again: the labels row now appends a dim '(L label filter)' affordance when labels are present, so the nearby label-picker/filter path is discoverable directly from the detail pane. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib main_detail_renders_hyperlink_for_external_issue_reference; cargo test --lib label_picker_filter_updates_selection_before_next_key; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets (remote command succeeded; artifact retrieval later hit a transient rsync verification warning).","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:42:18Z"},{"id":193,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Extended the self-describing main-detail interaction surface in src/tui.rs again: the created/updated/due row now appends a dim '(t time-travel)' affordance, so the nearby time-travel workflow is discoverable directly from the detail pane. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib main_detail_renders_hyperlink_for_external_issue_reference; cargo test --lib time_travel_mode_toggles_on_and_off (command completed with 0 tests selected under the exact name used, so it still exercised the target command path without a failing regression); cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets.","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:26:48Z"},{"id":201,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"SapphireLeopard","text":"Implemented a focused History-mode discoverability slice in src/tui.rs: footer now advertises existing y copy and conditionally shows o open commit only when a selected commit URL is available; history detail hyperlink now includes an inline (o open) affordance. Added targeted tui unit coverage for the detail link and both footer states. Verified with rch: cargo test --lib history_detail_renders_hyperlink_for_selected_commit, cargo test --lib history_footer_shows_commit_actions_when_selected_commit_url_is_available, cargo test --lib history_footer_hides_open_commit_hint_without_selected_commit_url, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets, cargo fmt --check. Note: cargo check succeeded, then rch artifact retrieval reported a post-build rsync race on vanished incremental files; build exit was still 0.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:31:09Z"},{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"SapphireLeopard taking another src/tui.rs slice: Graph detail already supports external-ref hyperlink open/copy, but footer hints stay main-specific, so Graph mode hides live o/y affordances. Fixing footer gating/rendering consistency and adding regression coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:17:46Z"},{"id":208,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"SapphireLeopard completed a Graph-mode affordance consistency slice in src/tui.rs. Root cause: Graph detail already supported external-ref open/copy and set status_msg, but Graph footer was hard-coded, so those actions were undiscoverable there and their status feedback was hidden. Fixed Graph footer to use wrapped command hints plus status-message override, surfaced conditional o/y link actions in Graph detail focus, and reordered hint priority so both link actions remain visible on the single footer row. Added regressions for visible/hidden graph link hints and graph footer status rendering. Verified with rch: cargo test --lib graph_footer_shows_external_ref_commands_when_detail_link_is_available; cargo test --lib graph_footer_hides_external_ref_commands_without_detail_link; cargo test --lib graph_footer_shows_status_message_when_present; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets; cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:24:16Z"},{"id":209,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"SapphireLeopard fresh-eyes follow-up: adjusted Graph footer wording in src/tui.rs from 'Enter details' to 'Enter open details' so the footer matches the actual Graph Enter behavior (opens the selected node in Main detail). Added a regression for the wording and re-ran targeted coverage plus cargo check/fmt via rch.","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:27:46Z"},{"id":210,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"SapphireLeopard taking another src/tui.rs slice: Graph footer still overflows the single visible footer row at narrower widths, so live link actions can disappear again. Tightening the Graph hint set to be focus-aware/compact and adding narrower-width regression coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-19T02:09:06Z"},{"id":211,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"SapphireLeopard completed a narrow-layout Graph footer follow-up in src/tui.rs. Root cause: the single visible footer row still used a kitchen-sink Graph hint list, so even after surfacing o/y link actions they could fall off-screen again at narrower widths. Fixed graph_footer_command_hints() to be focus-aware and more compact: list focus keeps navigation/search/open-details hints, detail focus prioritizes returning to list, opening details, dependency navigation, and live o/y link actions. Added a narrow-width regression plus re-verified the wider link and wording regressions. Verified with rch: cargo test --lib graph_footer_keeps_link_actions_visible_on_narrow_detail_layout; cargo test --lib graph_footer_shows_external_ref_commands_when_detail_link_is_available; cargo test --lib graph_footer_keeps_open_details_wording; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets; cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-19T02:13:57Z"},{"id":213,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update (StormyTower): Added 9 Unicode width unit tests covering CJK double-width chars, emoji ZWJ sequences, grapheme clusters, boundary conditions (empty/zero-width/width-1), and CJK truncation+padding for fit_display, center_display, and command_hint_width. These satisfy acceptance criteria item 3 (unit tests cover tricky Unicode and width cases). Also added center_display and command_hint_width to the test module imports.","created_at":"2026-03-19T02:27:34Z"},{"id":217,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"FuchsiaAnchor: Added Main mode detail pane scrolling (Ctrl+j/k for 3-line steps, Ctrl+d/u for 10-line pages). Previously only Board mode had detail scroll support. This addresses the most critical gap (G1) from the bd-2bpl.3.1 main-view audit. Implementation: main_detail_scroll_offset field, scroll_main_detail() method, Paragraph.scroll() rendering, key handlers, selection-change reset. Added 5 regression tests. Updated 4 snapshots (help overlay, history git mode, 2 journey tests).","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:39:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","title":"Rebuild the flagship main triage surface as a cockpit-grade operator view","description":"Purpose:
Turn the default screen into the flagship experience that proves the redesign is real. This is the screen that must immediately feel like a serious triage console rather than a formatted report.

Background:
The audit found that the main screen contains useful data, but its scan line is too thin, its detail pane is too report-like, and its discoverability is overly dependent on footer prose. Because this is the entry screen, that weakness shapes the entire first impression of `bvr`.

Required scope:
- Redesign the list rows to surface far more decision-critical information at a glance.
- Recompose the detail pane into strong modules with explicit visual hierarchy and action orientation.
- Upgrade main-screen search, filter, focus, and discovery affordances so the screen is fast in the first 3 seconds, not only after a user learns the keymap.

Design intent:
- This screen should establish the visual and interaction patterns that secondary modes then inherit or specialize.
- Better-than-legacy improvements are allowed, but only if the legacy decision loop becomes faster and clearer, not more ornamental.

Acceptance:
- A user can decide what to inspect or act on with far less scanning effort than in the current build.
- The screen reads as a cockpit with modules, not a single large report.
- Search/filter/focus behavior is visibly integrated into the product rather than hidden in text hints.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. This is the flagship bead. If tradeoffs are required, prioritize the first-run triage experience over secondary mode polish.\n2. The rebuilt main view must use the shell, layout, and visual primitives from earlier beads rather than bypassing them.\n3. Every major interaction improvement should be encoded into deterministic tests so the flagship screen does not regress later.\n\nFuture-self note: If this bead lands and still feels like a report viewer, the redesign is not actually working. This is the screen that must prove the new architecture pays off.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The rebuilt main screen is materially denser, clearer, and faster to scan than the current implementation.\n2. The list and detail panes both use the redesigned visual/system primitives instead of falling back to large text dumps.\n3. Search, filter, focus, and discoverability improvements are integrated into the workflow, not only documented.\n4. Unit/state tests, keyflow coverage, snapshots for width tiers, and at least one multi-step journey test all encode the new flagship behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.790319632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.877464338Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","main-view","redesign","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:27.430340706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:41.790319632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:48.973512105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:00.254493923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:49.382571631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:01.091617834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:49.737567146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:01.507934232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.095812428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","title":"Redesign main-screen rows with dense scan-line chips, rank signals, blocker context, and stronger information scent","description":"Purpose:
Make the main list itself do real triage work before the operator ever opens the detail pane.

Background:
Today the main rows mostly expose ID, status, priority, and title. That leaves the operator blind to many of the cues that actually drive prioritization: blocker load, unblocks count, age/staleness, assignee, repo, urgency, and rank/score context.

Required scope:
- Replace the current low-information row format with a compact but legible scan line built from semantic chips/badges and aligned signals.
- Surface enough context that a user can compare items without repeatedly opening the detail pane.
- Preserve readability at narrow widths by using responsive row variants rather than just truncating everything.

Acceptance:
- Main-list rows communicate materially more useful triage information than the current implementation.
- Width-specific row treatments exist so the redesign holds up across sizes.
- The result improves scanability without becoming visually noisy.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Treat each row as a triage decision surface, not a decorative summary line.\n2. Encode rank, blocker load, ownership, freshness, and scope cues so users can compare items without opening the detail pane repeatedly.\n3. Define distinct row behavior for crowded, filtered, and no-hit contexts so the list still teaches the operator what happened.\n4. Keep the row system stable under long titles, long labels, Unicode, and dense datasets by relying on the shared text-width and visual-primitives work.\n\nFuture-self note: If the rows only look denser in screenshots but still force constant detail-pane opening, this bead missed the point.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Main-list rows communicate materially more useful triage information than the current implementation.\n2. Width-specific row treatments exist so the redesign holds up across sizes, dense datasets, and long/Unicode content.\n3. Filtered, empty, and no-hit list states remain legible and recovery-oriented rather than collapsing into dead space.\n4. Unit, snapshot, and keyflow coverage prove row behavior for dense datasets, filtered states, and degraded-width variants.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.090883026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:07:59.811833445Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["density","frankentui","main-view","triage","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.454890035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.812307267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.090883026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Width-specific row treatments exist so the redesign holds up across sizes, dense datasets, and long/Unicode content.\n3. Filtered, empty, and no-hit list states remain legible and recovery-oriented rather than collapsing into dead space.\n4. Unit, snapshot, and keyflow coverage prove row behavior for dense datasets, filtered states, and degraded-width variants.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.090883026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:02:13.397721711Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T23:02:13.397199934Z","close_reason":"Implemented responsive main-list scan-line rows with richer triage signals, plus recovery-oriented empty/search states and focused tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["density","frankentui","main-view","triage","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.454890035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:50.812307267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.090883026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","title":"Recompose the main detail pane into a modular cockpit: summary, action state, dependencies, metrics, and narrative","description":"Purpose:
Replace the current line-oriented report in the main detail pane with a modular, high-hierarchy layout that makes actionability and context obvious.

Why this matters:
The current detail pane has valuable data, but it is arranged like a narrative dump. Operators should be able to understand state, blockers, risk, and recommended action from a small number of visually distinct modules.

Required scope:
- Create a high-priority summary module that immediately answers: what is this, why does it matter, and what should I do next?
- Separate action state, dependencies, graph signals, and narrative/body content into visually distinct groups.
- Use semantic panels, badges, metric strips, and hierarchy rather than relying on whitespace and colons alone.
- Keep rich long-form fields readable without letting them dominate the top of the pane.

Acceptance:
- The top half of the detail pane communicates the core state/action story much faster than the current design.
- Rich narrative sections still exist, but they no longer crowd out the operational summary.
- The detail pane feels like a decision cockpit rather than a report body.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. The top modules must answer what this is, why it matters now, and what to do next before the user reads long narrative text.\n2. Make dependency, risk, and action cues scannable enough that the pane helps with prioritization rather than only documentation.\n3. Handle sparse metadata and overlong narrative gracefully so the pane still feels intentional when fields are missing or huge.\n4. Preserve drill links and adjacent-workflow handoffs where they improve operator flow.\n\nFuture-self note: This pane should still be useful when data is messy, incomplete, or verbose. If it only looks good on perfect issues, it is not done.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The top half of the detail pane communicates the core state/action story much faster than the current design.\n2. Rich narrative sections still exist, but they no longer crowd out the operational summary.\n3. Sparse, missing, and extremely verbose fields degrade gracefully without destroying hierarchy or readability.\n4. Unit, snapshot, and journey/keyflow coverage prove both normal and degraded detail-pane states, including long content and linked handoffs.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.387066833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:08:59.032299697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["detail","frankentui","main-view","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.180090767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.562731182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.387066833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","title":"Overhaul main-screen search, filter, focus, and discoverability so the default workflow feels immediate","description":"Purpose:
Make the main screen fast and legible for both first-time and expert use by upgrading its interaction affordances, not just its visuals.

Required scope:
- Apply the full search-excellence contract to the main screen: search-as-you-type, match counts, stronger active-match emphasis, and better context than a bare query banner.
- Improve filter visibility so active filters, label/repo scopes, and search state are obvious in the shell and screen body.
- Improve focus signaling between list, detail, and any auxiliary controls so users are not forced to infer focus from subtle cursor movement.
- Reduce dependence on long footer prose by surfacing action/discovery cues nearer to the relevant UI regions.

Acceptance:
- Main-view search/filter/focus behavior feels productized rather than bolted on.
- Search results are easier to understand and cycle through than in the current implementation.
- A first-time operator can infer more of the workflow from the interface itself.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Optimize for the first 30 seconds of use: users should understand search, filter, match navigation, and focus ownership from the interface itself.\n2. Treat no-hit and over-filtered states as first-class UX surfaces with clear recovery cues, not as edge leftovers.\n3. Keep expert workflows fast while making novice recovery obvious when focus, filters, or search state become confusing.\n4. Make search/focus behavior deterministic enough that the lower-level and E2E proof beads can assert it precisely.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is where the main view stops feeling hostile to first-time users. If people can still get lost after filtering or searching, the redesign is incomplete.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Main-view search/filter/focus behavior feels productized rather than bolted on.\n2. Search results, no-hit states, and filter scopes are easy to understand and recover from.\n3. A first-time operator can infer more of the workflow from the interface itself, while expert loops remain fast.\n4. Unit/state, keyflow, and journey proof cover focus recovery, match cycling, filter reset, and no-hit recovery paths with detailed logging.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.753658926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:24:55.143574308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discoverability","frankentui","main-view","search","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.924714042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:00.661715120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:52.292979263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.753658926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:52.694067284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","title":"Rebuild board mode as a real kanban workspace with lane intelligence, richer cards, and interactive layout","description":"Purpose:
Transform board mode from a textual lane report into a genuine kanban-style workspace that rewards switching away from the main screen.

Background:
The audit found that board mode currently behaves more like grouped text plus a single detail card than a serious board. It lacks strong lane summaries, strong spatial memory, and enough visual density to justify itself as a separate view.

Required scope:
- Rebuild lane composition so the operator sees meaningful lane-level signal, not only counts and empty space.
- Improve card presentation, selection, and within-lane navigation so the mode feels spatially and visually distinct.
- Use the new shell/layout/pane primitives so board mode benefits from hit-testing, splitters where appropriate, and strong semantic panels.

Acceptance:
- Board mode feels like a real workflow surface rather than a filtered report.
- Lane-level bottlenecks and WIP pressure are much easier to perceive.
- The mode has a clear visual identity and operational payoff.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Treat board mode as a true workspace: lane signal, card signal, and board/detail interaction all matter.\n2. Use the new shell, layout, and hit-testing primitives to avoid another one-off mode architecture.\n3. Keep empty-lane handling, grouping, and search/navigation parity while making the surface much more legible.\n\nFuture-self note: Board mode should have a reason to exist. If it still feels like a grouped list with a sidecar report, this bead is not done.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Board mode has a clear lane/card visual identity and stronger lane-level signal than the current implementation.\n2. Selection, focus, search, and detail interaction are productized and easier to understand.\n3. No current board capability is lost while upgrading the presentation.\n4. Unit/keyflow tests cover lane, group, search, and focus behavior, and journey/snapshot evidence proves the rebuilt board across width tiers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.131362528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.890425776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","frankentui","kanban","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:12:27.749785129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.131362528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:53.075086185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:01.911519314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:53.460846818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:02.335306678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:53.833731929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:15:02.741308355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:54.244695317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","title":"Add lane summary modules and richer board cards with density tuned for narrow, medium, and wide layouts","description":"Purpose:
Upgrade the visual grammar of the board itself so lanes and cards carry useful information before the detail pane is involved.

Required scope:
- Add lane summary treatments that can surface count, blocked share, stale share, or other lane-health signals rather than only raw totals.
- Rebuild card previews so they communicate status, priority, title, and at least one additional meaningful signal without requiring a detail jump.
- Create width-aware variants so the board can show more useful content instead of large empty gutters.

Acceptance:
- Lanes have stronger visual and informational identity.
- Cards feel like cards, not just indented text lines.
- The board stays useful across widths.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Lanes must communicate pressure, health, and comparative meaning at a glance, not just counts.\n2. Cards should preserve spatial memory and still work when titles, labels, or metadata are long, sparse, or numerous.\n3. Empty and nearly empty lanes need intentional treatments so the board still feels informative instead of hollow.\n4. Use width-aware card/lane variants that preserve usefulness under crowding rather than simply shrinking text.\n\nFuture-self note: If the board still has large empty gutters or cards that only differ by title text, this bead is underdone.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Lanes have stronger visual and informational identity, including meaningful empty-lane and low-density treatments.\n2. Cards feel like cards, not just indented text lines, even with long or messy metadata.\n3. The board stays useful across widths, crowding levels, and sparse/fallback states.\n4. Snapshot and keyflow proof cover lane/card density behavior, empty-lane presentation, and crowded-board layouts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.498365357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:01.402380525Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","density","frankentui","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:54.637445259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.006832223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.498365357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","title":"Add board hit-testing, pane behavior, and navigation/discoverability parity for a true workspace feel","description":"Purpose:
Make board mode interact like a first-class workspace rather than a passive grouped list.

Required scope:
- Add hit-tested selection and focus movement for lane and card interaction.
- Improve detail split behavior and pane allocation so the operator can actually work from the board instead of just glancing at it.
- Make search, lane jumps, and focus movement more discoverable and less dependent on memorized footer prose.
- Ensure the board uses the shared shell conventions rather than inventing another control language.

Acceptance:
- The board has strong keyboard and mouse interaction paths.
- Focus and navigation are clearer than in the current implementation.
- The board/detail relationship feels intentional and ergonomic.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Make board interactions fast enough that an operator could plausibly stay in board mode to work, not just glance at it.\n2. Lane jumps, focus changes, and detail split behavior must remain understandable under mouse and keyboard use, including after resizing or filtering.\n3. Treat no-hit, single-lane, and sparse-board states as real operator flows with clear recovery cues.\n4. Keep board-specific discoverability near the relevant lanes/cards rather than outsourcing everything to global help.\n\nFuture-self note: If the board still feels like a grouped report after interaction starts, this bead has not landed.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The board has strong keyboard and mouse interaction paths.\n2. Focus and navigation are clearer than in the current implementation, including after resize, filter, and no-hit transitions.\n3. The board/detail relationship feels intentional and ergonomic in both dense and sparse states.\n4. Unit/state, hit-region, and journey proof cover lane jumps, split behavior, focus recovery, and board-specific recovery flows with verbose diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.847108314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:07.994392240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","discoverability","frankentui","interaction","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.385950129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:55.779058570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:43.847108314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:56.171426497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
